verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B is a component of the mitochondrial ATP synthase complex that predominantly localizes to the inner mitochondrial membrane matrix side. However, documented evidence establishes that the complete F1Fo-ATP synthase complex, including ATP5F1B, undergoes translocation to the plasma membrane in specific cell types (hepatocytes, endothelial cells, adipocytes, cancer cells). This translocation occurs via a defined mechanism: assembly in mitochondria, transport along microtubules (via DRP1/KIF5B), and mitochondrial-plasma membrane fusion. The surface pool exhibits catalytic activity and is confirmed by multiple orthogonal methods. Surface localization is tissue/cell-type restricted and not the dominant baseline pool, qualifying this as contextual rather than yes.
